from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single, fluent sentence.
    When all triples share the same subject they are merged into one complex
    description; otherwise each triple is rendered as an independent clause.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to clean object string (remove surrounding quotes or angle brackets)
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        obj = obj.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        if obj.startswith("<") and obj.endswith(">"):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural language
    _templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "features {} architecture",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction started in {}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {}",
        "floorCount": "has {} floors",
        "location": "is located at {}",
        "cost": "costs {}",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of {} square metres",
        "architect": "was designed by {}",
        "owner": "is owned by {}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {}",
        "height": "reaches a height of {} metres",
        "buildingType": "is a {}",
        "developer": "was developed by {}",
        "tenant": "hosts {} as a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {}",
        "country": "lies in {}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {}",
        "address": "has the address {}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {}",
        "bedCount": "has {} beds",
        "region": "is in the region of {}",
        "state": "is located in {}",
        "website": "has a website at {}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was built in {}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building {}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {}",
        "leader": "is led by {}",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor {}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {}",
        "capital": "has capital {}",
        "language": "speaks {}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {}",
        "ethnicGroup": "is home to {}",
        "origin": "originates from {}",
        "significantProject": "is noted for the project {}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {}",
        "demonym": "its people are called {}",
        "numberOfRooms": "contains {} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {}",
        "architecture": "exhibits {} architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {}",
    }

    def _phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        pred = triple.predicate
        obj = _clean(triple.object)
        tmpl = _templates.get(pred)
        if tmpl:
            return tmpl.format(obj)
        # fallback – make predicate readable
        pred_readable = pred.replace('_', ' ')
        return f"{pred_readable} {obj}"

    # Determine whether all triples refer to the same subject
    subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
    if len(subjects) == 1:
        subject = triples[0].subject
        phrases = [_phrase(t) for t in triples]
        if not phrases:
            return subject
        # join with commas and an Oxford “and”
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            description = phrases[0]
        else:
            description = ', '.join(phrases[:-1]) + f", and {phrases[-1]}"
        return f"{subject} {description}."
    else:
        # Different subjects – render each triple as its own clause
        clauses = [f"{t.subject} {_phrase(t)}" for t in triples]
        return '; '.join(clauses) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single, fluent sentence.
      If all triples share the same subject, the sentence lists the attributes
      of that subject; otherwise each triple is expressed separately.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Determine whether all triples refer to the same subject
      subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
      if len(subjects) == 1:
          subject = triples[0].subject
          parts = []
          for t in triples:
              # make predicate more readable
              pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
              parts.append(f"{pred} {t.object}")
          # Join attributes with commas and an 'and' before the last item
          if len(parts) > 1:
              attrs = ', '.join(parts[:-1]) + f", and {parts[-1]}"
          else:
              attrs = parts[0]
          return f"{subject} has {attrs}."
      else:
          # Different subjects – produce a short clause for each triple
          clauses = []
          for t in triples:
              pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
              clauses.append(f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}")
